vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1675: feat: Panopticon-side compaction (resume --compact) — recover wedged agents without the harness /compact deadlock

plan.title: Panopticon-side compaction (resume --compact) — recover wedged agents without the harness /compact deadlock
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: When a gpt-5.5/CLIProxy work agent overflows its context window, the harness's own /compact deadlocks: it re-sends the entire over-limit transcript to produce the summary, which re-400s with 'input exceeds the context window'. The deacon's only working escalation is /clear, which drops the whole transcript and loses all in-session reasoning. PAN-1672 fix #1 lowered the window to fire proactive compaction earlier, but the underlying recovery still depends on the deadlock-prone harness compaction path.
narratives.Proposal: Wire Panopticon's EXISTING out-of-band compaction into the work-agent resume path. compactConversationNative() already reads an agent's JSONL, runs a chunked smart summary via the configured compaction_model (independent of the wedged session), and appends a compact_boundary so `claude --resume` continues from a small window — this is proven for conversations (manual_compact_mode='panopticon-native'). Add one primitive compactAgentSession(agentId) wrapping it for work agents; expose it as resumeAgent({ compact:true }); surface it as `pan resume --compact`, a dashboard 'Resume (compact)' action, and the deacon's fresh-overflow recovery tier (falling back to /clear only if Panopticon-side compaction fails). Because summarization is out-of-band and chunked, the over-limit transcript is never re-sent through the deadlocked model — the deadlock is structurally impossible.

autoDecisions.summary: Reuse compactConversationNative()/generateSmartSummary() rather than writing new summarization.
autoDecisions.rationale: The exact out-of-band, chunked, boundary-appending mechanism the issue describes already exists and is proven for conversations. Reimplementing it would duplicate ~1100 lines and risk re-introducing the deadlock. Issue is a wiring task.

autoDecisions.summary: Scope the deacon change to the wedged-RECOVERY tier only; leave the proactive 85% tier on harness /compact.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue Surface section names the recovery tier specifically ('use this as its escalation instead of the harness /compact, falling back to /clear'). Proactive compaction fires below the ceiling where /compact still works; converting it is a separate follow-up.

autoDecisions.summary: Frontend action goes on IssueAgentCard (work agents) only, not SpecialistAgentCard.
autoDecisions.rationale: The wedge is a gpt-5.5/CLIProxy work-agent problem (agent-pan-1641 live observation). Specialists rarely overflow and are not the issue's target.

autoDecisions.summary: compactAgentSession returns { compacted, error } and never throws; compaction runs before the zombie-kill with early-return-on-failure.
autoDecisions.rationale: Preserves the live wedged session for the /clear fallback when compaction fails, matching the issue's fallback requirement, and keeps the primitive trivially testable.

items.title: Add compactAgentSession(agentId) primitive — out-of-band native compaction of a work agent's JSONL

narrative.Action: In src/lib/agents.ts add and export `compactAgentSession(agentId: string): Promise<{ compacted: boolean; error?: string }>`. It resolves the agent state (getAgentStateSync) and saved Claude session id (getLatestSessionIdSync), derives the JSONL path with sessionFilePath(workspace, sessionId), and runs compaction by DYNAMICALLY importing compactConversationNative from ../dashboard/server/services/conversation-compaction.js (mirrors the deacon's dynamic-import pattern to avoid the known circular dependency — see hazard H1). Call compactConversationNative(sessionFile) WITHOUT a conversationName so no conversation.compacting events fire for a work-agent JSONL (H5). Catch all errors and return { compacted:false, error } — never throw. If no workspace or no saved sessionId, return { compacted:false, error } without attempting compaction (H4).

subItems.title: compactAgentSession is exported from src/lib/agents.ts with signature (agentId) => Promise<{ compacted: boolean; error?: string }> and never throws (all failure paths return { compacted:false, error }).

It resolves the SAME session file the harness resumes from: sessionFilePath(agentState.workspace, getLatestSessionIdSync(agentId)); when sessionId or workspace is missing it returns { compacted:false } without calling compaction.
compactConversationNative is loaded via dynamic import (no new top-level import of conversation-compaction in agents.ts) and called WITHOUT a conversationName argument.
npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass.

items.title: Add compact option to resumeAgent() — compact JSONL before relaunch, fail-safe before zombie-kill

narrative.Action: Extend resumeAgent(agentId, message?, opts?) in src/lib/agents.ts with `compact?: boolean` on opts. When opts.compact is true, at the very START of the try block (BEFORE the existing zombie tmux-session kill at ~line 4188) call compactAgentSession(normalizedId). If it returns { compacted:false, error }, return { success:false, error: `Panopticon-side compaction failed: ${error}` } immediately — do NOT kill the session or relaunch, so the live wedged session survives for a /clear fallback (H2/H3). If compaction succeeds, continue the normal kill + `claude --resume` relaunch unchanged. When opts.compact is falsy, behaviour is byte-for-byte identical to today.

subItems.title: resumeAgent opts accepts compact?: boolean; passing { compact:true } invokes compactAgentSession before the zombie-session kill.
When compaction fails, resumeAgent returns { success:false, error } WITHOUT killing the tmux session or building a launcher (the wedged session is left live for /clear fallback).
When opts.compact is omitted/false, the resume path is unchanged (no compaction call).
npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass.

narrative.Action: In src/cli/index.ts add `.option('--compact', 'Run Panopticon-side out-of-band compaction on the agent JSONL before resuming (recovers context-window-wedged agents)')` to the `resume <id>` command. In src/cli/commands/resume.ts add `compact?: boolean` to ResumeOptions and pass it through to resumeAgent(id, undefined, { ..., compact: options.compact === true }). On a compaction failure, surface the returned error and exit non-zero. No pan-resume wrapper skill exists, so no skill needs updating.

subItems.title: `pan resume <id> --compact` calls resumeAgent with { compact:true }; `pan resume <id>` (no flag) calls it without compact.
`pan resume --help` lists the --compact flag with a description.
A compaction failure prints the error and exits with a non-zero code.

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts, postAgentResumeRoute: read `compact` from the JSON body alongside message/model/harness, and include it in resumeOpts passed to resumeAgent (resumeOpts = { ...model, ...harness, ...(compact ? { compact:true } : {}) }). Keep the existing lifecycle gate and event emission unchanged.

subItems.title: POST /api/agents/:id/resume with body { compact:true } calls resumeAgent with opts.compact === true.
Body without compact resumes exactly as before (compact undefined).
npm run typecheck passes; npm run build of the server succeeds.

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/IssueAgentCard.tsx extend the resumeAgent() fetch helper to accept an optional { compact?: boolean } and include it in the POST body. Add a secondary 'Resume (compact)' control next to the existing Resume button (or a menu item) that calls the mutation with compact:true. Show it for resumable agents; a tooltip should explain it recovers a context-window-wedged agent via out-of-band compaction. Reuse the existing resume mutation's loading/error handling (showAlert on failure).

subItems.title: IssueAgentCard renders a 'Resume (compact)' action for resumable work agents that POSTs /api/agents/:id/resume with { compact:true }.
The plain Resume button still POSTs without compact; both share loading/error handling.
Frontend typecheck/lint pass and the action has an accessible label/tooltip describing the out-of-band recovery.

items.title: Deacon: use Panopticon-side compaction for the fresh-overflow recovery tier (fallback to /clear)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents(), the FRESH-overflow branch for agent-* sessions (currently `await sendKeys(sessionName, '/compact')` followed by setting contextOverflowRecoveryState phase 'compact', around lines 1052-1074) is replaced: call resumeAgent(sessionName, undefined, { compact:true }). On result.success, set contextOverflowRecoveryState phase 'compact' (so the existing 'previous tier cleared the overflow' branch nudges the resumed agent to continue) and emit a 'recovering via Panopticon-side compaction' activity entry; push an action string. On failure (result.success false), DO NOT record a compact attempt — fall straight through to the existing /clear + reseed tier in the SAME patrol pass (so a failed native compaction immediately escalates to /clear, matching the issue's fallback semantics). The /clear tier, the MAX_CONTEXT_CLEAR_ATTEMPTS ceiling, the markWorkspaceStuck escalation, and the contextSaturatedAt bookkeeping are all unchanged. Restrict native compaction to agent-* sessions (specialists/planning keep the old /compact behaviour). Leave a code comment at maybeProactivelyCompactContext noting the proactive 85% tier still uses harness /compact by design (PAN-1675, H6).

subItems.title: On a fresh context-overflow for an agent-* session, the deacon calls resumeAgent(session, undefined, { compact:true }) instead of sendKeys('/compact').
When resumeAgent({compact:true}) fails, the same patrol pass falls through to the existing /clear + reseed tier (no compact attempt recorded, /clear sent against the still-live session).

On success, contextOverflowRecoveryState records phase 'compact' so the next patrol's 'overflow cleared' branch nudges the resumed agent; an activity entry naming Panopticon-side compaction is emitted.
The /clear ceiling, markWorkspaceStuck escalation, contextSaturatedAt bookkeeping, and the proactive 85% tier are unchanged; non-agent (specialist/planning) sessions keep the old /compact path.

items.title: Unit test: compactAgentSession resolves the right JSONL and returns a non-throwing result

narrative.Action: Add a Vitest unit test for compactAgentSession that mocks compactConversationNative (the dynamically-imported module) and the agent-state/session-id resolvers. Assert: (1) it calls compactConversationNative with sessionFilePath(workspace, sessionId) and no conversationName; (2) it returns { compacted:true } on success; (3) it returns { compacted:false, error } (never throws) when compactConversationNative rejects; (4) it returns { compacted:false } without calling compaction when no sessionId/workspace is resolvable.

subItems.title: Test asserts compactConversationNative is called with the agent's resolved sessionFilePath and no conversationName.
Test asserts a rejecting compactConversationNative yields { compacted:false, error } and does not throw.
Test asserts missing sessionId/workspace short-circuits to { compacted:false } with no compaction call. Test passes under npm test.

items.title: Unit test: deacon fresh-overflow tier uses native compaction and falls back to /clear

narrative.Action: Add a Vitest test (alongside the existing deacon/context-overflow tests) that drives checkApiErrorAgents with a mocked agent-* session whose tmux tail contains a context-overflow error. Mock resumeAgent and sendKeys. Assert: (1) fresh overflow → resumeAgent called with { compact:true } and NO sendKeys('/compact'); (2) when resumeAgent resolves { success:false }, sendKeys('/clear') + reseed is sent in the same pass; (3) when resumeAgent resolves { success:true }, no /clear is sent and contextOverflowRecoveryState records phase 'compact'. Use fake timers if any settle/cooldown windows are involved (per the fake-timers rule).

subItems.title: Test: fresh agent-* overflow triggers resumeAgent({compact:true}) and never sendKeys('/compact').
Test: resumeAgent failure → /clear + reseed sent in the same patrol pass.
Test: resumeAgent success → no /clear; recovery state phase is 'compact'. Suite uses fake timers for any delay windows and passes under npm test.

items.title: Document `resume --compact` and the deacon native-compaction recovery tier

narrative.Action: Document the new recovery path where the existing context-overflow / compaction behaviour is described (the doc that covers the deacon overflow tiers and conversation-compaction — locate via grep for 'panopticon-native' / 'context-window overflow' in docs/). Add a short section: `pan resume <id> --compact` and the dashboard 'Resume (compact)' action run out-of-band native compaction (compactConversationNative via compactAgentSession) on the agent JSONL before `claude --resume`; the deacon's fresh-overflow recovery tier now uses this instead of harness /compact, falling back to /clear only if Panopticon-side compaction fails; the proactive 85% tier still uses harness /compact by design. No code behaviour changes in this bead.

subItems.title: The relevant existing doc gains a section describing pan resume --compact, the dashboard action, and the deacon recovery-tier change with the /clear fallback.
The doc states the proactive 85% tier intentionally still uses harness /compact (scope note).
